methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $2.6B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
AbCellera operates in Biotech & Health and is currently public-stage. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage companies: Freenome ($2.6B), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), and Alto Neuroscience ($529M).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a $1.8B implied valuation. This reflects the median across the three public-stage biotech comparables, with a range spanning $529M at the low end to $2.6B at the high end. The Public-Comp Anchoring method cannot be applied because AbCellera's revenue is not on file; without disclosed revenue, price-to-sales multiples derived from the three public biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L)—which trade at a 6.1x median P/S—cannot be anchored to the target.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $2.1B, with a median of $1.8B. This range rests on a single applicable method, creating meaningful uncertainty around the point estimate. Key sensitivities include AbCellera's actual revenue run rate relative to peers and relative profitability or cash burn profiles, neither of which are quantified in available data. The width of the peer range ($529M to $2.6B) underscores stage and sector heterogeneity even within the same classification.
